(Dr. Akhilesh Tiwari)
Dean, Academics
MITS- Deemed L'niversity
Page 1 of 2
Important Instructions
(First Year - All UG & PG Programmes)
1. Students must regularly check their attendance status on Integrated University Management System (IUMS) / MOODLE and also with their
class coordinators. Those not having attendance as per the ordinance of MITS-DU, will not be allowed to appear in the evaluation.
2. All the First year students are expected to mandatorily register for Novel Engaging Courses (NEC) (Holistic Development Courses as per NEP
2020), in their First & Second semester and Skill Internship Program (Soft Skill Based) at the end of First semester.
3. Students are expected to submit all the assignments and appear for all weekly quizzes for their continuous assessment.
4. Students must attend "Additional Classes" conducted by the concerning faculty for clearing the doubts and discussing other academic issues,
if needed.
5. For each student, it is compulsory to appear in two Minor Evaluations. Minor evaluation marks will be awarded by taking the sum of two
minor evaluation marks. First two units will be covered under Minor- l Evaluation and next two units i.e. unit 3 and unit 4 will be covered
under Minor -II Evaluation.
6. Make-up Evaluation will be conducted only for those students who could not appear in either Minor-I or Minor-II Evaluations or both, due
to some valid/bonafide reasons with prior permission from the Class Coordinators & Head of respective department/centre/school. Full
syllabus will be covered under Make-up evaluation.
7. Major Evaluation (i.e. Final Exam) fee will be deposited during Semester Registration itself, but to be eligible for Major Evaluation,
requirements specified in the ordinance of MITS-DU are to be complied.
8. Pre-Result Review (Major Evaluations): In case of any discrepancies, the students may apply for review.
9. Grade Moderation: Statistical System of Grading (called Relative Grading) has been implemented in the institute for the award of grades
(Except for M.E./ M.Tech./MUP Programmes).
10. Summer Semester is an optional semester, provisioned/conducted at the end of each year to enable the students for clearing backlog courses
/'
(if any) of preceding semesters and also to makeup improve the CCPA (only if the candidate has failed to score minimum CGPA of 5.0, which
is the mandate requirement of Ordinance).
II. In situation, if result put on hold, such students will be provisionally promoted to next higher semester / year with undertaking to the effect
that if they fail, their provisional registration will be treated as cancelled. Attendance during the period of provisional registration will be
counted for fulfilling attendance requirements.
(Dr. Akhilesh Tiwari)
Dean, Academics - MITS- Deemed University
Page 2 of 2
